sample.assertion: Output format control is the set of prompting techniques that declare the exact shape of a response and hold the model to it
LLM trained on enormous amounts of human-written text — articles, books, forum posts, code
Python program expects JSON but model returns prose sentence with product name and price
Format specification is writing a clear, explicit instruction in your prompt that tells the model the exact shape
Format example is the format-focused application of few-shot prompting from topic 13.3
Format drift is when a model response does not match the format you declared
Format drift happens because model treats format instructions as strong suggestions, not hard rules
Output validator pattern is checking format after a response and retrying once before returning a fallback
Without suppression instruction, many models wrap JSON in a sentence or code fence, breaking json.loads()
Automated data extraction uses JSON format instructions to pull structured fields directly into a database

faithfulness.notes: All sampled assertions trace directly to corpus sections 4, 5.1–5.7, and 7.
